title: Abu Dhabi Lab earns WHO accreditation for measles & rubella | HealthCare Middle East & Africa - No.1 Health & Pharma Manufacturing Industry Publication
summary: The designation highlights Abu Dhabi’s expanding role in global health security, particularly in strengthening systems that detect, track, and respond to infectious disease threats.
thbAction: Pitch THB's diagnostics CRM to help labs manage doctor outreach, collection-center expansion, and B2B referral relationships while improving conversion and retention.
contentHtml:
UAE—The Abu Dhabi Public Health Centre has announced that its Infectious Diseases Reference Laboratory, based at Sheikh Khalifa Medical City, has received accreditation from the World Health Organization as a national reference laboratory for measles and rubella.

 The recognition places the facility within the WHO global laboratory network that monitors and supports the control of both diseases across different regions.

The designation highlights Abu Dhabi’s expanding role in global health security, particularly in strengthening systems that detect, track, and respond to infectious disease threats.

It also reflects growing international confidence in the emirate’s diagnostic capacity and public health infrastructure.

Strengthening disease detection and surveillance systems

The accreditation reinforces the laboratory’s ability to accurately identify measles and rubella cases while improving early detection and response capabilities.

In addition, it supports ongoing national and regional efforts to eliminate both diseases through stronger surveillance and coordinated public health action.

The laboratory uses advanced serological and molecular testing methods that allow faster confirmation of suspected cases.

As a result, health authorities can respond more quickly, reduce transmission risks, and improve data-driven decision-making across the health system.

WHO evaluation confirms global standards compliance

The recognition followed a detailed external assessment conducted by WHO experts.

The evaluation reviewed multiple aspects of laboratory performance, including diagnostic accuracy, biosafety protocols, and overall quality management systems.

This assessment confirmed that the facility consistently meets international standards required for reference laboratories.

This positions Abu Dhabi as a reliable contributor to global infectious disease monitoring and verification processes.

Integration into a smart health ecosystem

The laboratory operates within Abu Dhabi’s broader smart health infrastructure, where diagnostic data feeds directly into coordinated public health systems.

Through digital integration, laboratory findings are transformed into actionable insights that support rapid surveillance and response.

This system connects with national coordination platforms, including the Unified Medical Operations Command Center, enabling authorities to align responses and strengthen preparedness for emerging health risks.

Leadership highlights public health commitment

Speaking on the accreditation, His Excellency Dr. Rashid Al Suwaidi, Director General of the Abu Dhabi Public Health Centre, said the recognition reflects Abu Dhabi’s commitment to building a proactive and resilient health system.

He noted that continued investment in infrastructure, technology, and skilled professionals has strengthened the emirate’s ability to meet global health challenges.

He also emphasized that joining the WHO measles and rubella laboratory network enhances Abu Dhabi’s role as a trusted international partner in disease surveillance and response.

Expanding national laboratory capabilities

The Infectious Diseases Reference Laboratory also serves as a WHO-recognised National Influenza Centre, reinforcing its broader mandate in infectious disease diagnostics.

Over time, it has expanded its capabilities in molecular testing and genomic sequencing, supporting both prevention and outbreak response strategies across the UAE.

Through sustained investment in laboratory systems and public health readiness, Abu Dhabi continues to develop a more responsive healthcare model designed to improve detection, enhance coordination, and support long-term disease control efforts.
